Defendant Autocar, Llc'S Motion For Protective Order [Per Order Filed 7/24/2020]
Off Calendar Aug-17-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: Defendant Autocar, LLC's Motion For Protective Order.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The motion of Autocar, LLC ("Autocar") is DENIED as follows:

Autocar has not met its burden of establishing good cause for issuance of a protective order. First, based on the record before the court, the subject discovery appears relevant to the subject matter of the dispute as framed by the pleadings and reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. Second, Autocar has not satisfactorily shown that the burden, expense, or intrusiveness involved in the discovery procedure clearly outweighs the likelihood that the information sought will lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. It has not provided specific facts demonstrating undue burden and justifying the relief sought. In particular, Autocar has not satisfied the procedural and evidentiary requirements to protect its alleged trade secrets from disclosure. (Evid. Code Sec 1060-61; CCP Secs 2030.090(b)(6), 2031.060(b)(5); Stadish v. Sup.Ct. (Southern Calif. Gas Co.) (1999) 71 Cal.App 4th 1130, 1144-1145 (procedure required by Evidence Code section 1061 in criminal cases held applicable to civil cases).) To be clear, the denial of Autocar's motion does not preclude it from making appropriate objections in its responses to the subject discovery.

The court suggests the parties continue their prior discussions to agree on the terms of a stipulated protective order to facilitate their discovery initiatives.

Tanganyika Gordon's request for sanctions is denied because the existence of a draft stipulated protective order is evidence the parties satisfactorily met & conferred prior to the filing of the motion.

Prevailing party Tanganyika Gordon is required to prepare a proposed order and e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at pvestal@nvlawllp.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Vestal; Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/JPT)
